maml:description.maml:para: Converts DSC v1 compiled MOF text to a DSC v3 configuration document.

Reads the text of a DSC v1 compiled MOF file and converts it to a DSC v3 `DscConfigDocument` object. Each resource instance in the MOF becomes a resource entry in the configuration document. Use the `-AsJson` switch to receive the output as a JSON string instead of an object.
Pipe the output of `Get-Content` on a compiled `.mof` file to this cmdlet.
If set, output JSON text instead of a `DscConfigDocument` object.
The MOF text to convert. Pipe the output of `Get-Content` on a compiled `.mof` file.
If set, output JSON text instead of a `DscConfigDocument` object.
The MOF text to convert. Pipe the output of `Get-Content` on a compiled `.mof` file.
The compiled MOF text, typically piped from `Get-Content`.
When `-AsJson` is not specified, returns a `DscConfigDocument` object representing the DSC v3 configuration.
When `-AsJson` is specified, returns the configuration as a JSON string.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1 - Convert a compiled MOF to a configuration object ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Get-Content -Path ./localhost.mof | ConvertFrom-DscConfigurationMof ```
Reads a compiled MOF file and returns a `DscConfigDocument` object.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2 - Convert a compiled MOF to JSON ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Get-Content -Path ./localhost.mof | ConvertFrom-DscConfigurationMof -AsJson ```
Reads a compiled MOF file and returns the DSC v3 configuration as a JSON string.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3 - Save the converted configuration to a YAML file ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Get-Content -Path ./localhost.mof | ConvertFrom-DscConfigurationMof -AsJson | Set-Content -Path ./configuration.dsc.json ```
Converts a compiled MOF to JSON and writes it to a file.

maml:description.maml:para: Converts a MOF schema class definition to a JSON Schema string.

Reads the text of a DSC v1 `.schema.mof` file and converts each class definition to a JSON Schema document. The output is a JSON string that describes the resource properties, types, and metadata from the original MOF schema.
Pipe the output of `Get-Content` on a `.schema.mof` file to this cmdlet.
The MOF schema text to convert. Pipe the output of `Get-Content` on a `.schema.mof` file.
The MOF schema text to convert. Pipe the output of `Get-Content` on a `.schema.mof` file.
The MOF schema text, typically piped from `Get-Content`.
Returns a JSON Schema string representing the converted MOF schema.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1 - Convert a schema MOF to JSON Schema ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Get-Content -Path ./MSFT_xWebsite.schema.mof | ConvertFrom-DscSchemaMof ```
Reads a schema MOF file and returns the equivalent JSON Schema as a string.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2 - Save the JSON Schema to a file ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Get-Content -Path ./MSFT_xWebsite.schema.mof | ConvertFrom-DscSchemaMof | Set-Content -Path ./MSFT_xWebsite.schema.json ```
Converts a schema MOF and writes the JSON Schema to a file.

maml:description.maml:para: Imports adapted resource manifest objects from `.dsc.adaptedResource.json` files.

Reads one or more `.dsc.adaptedResource.json` files and returns `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` objects. This is the inverse of serializing a manifest with `.ToJson()` — it allows you to load existing adapted resource manifests for inspection, modification, or inclusion in a resource manifest list via `New-DscResourceManifest`.
The path to a `.dsc.adaptedResource.json` file. Accepts pipeline input.
The path to a `.dsc.adaptedResource.json` file. Accepts pipeline input.
The path to a `.dsc.adaptedResource.json` file, typically piped from `Get-ChildItem`.
Returns a `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` object for each file. The object has `.ToJson()` and `.ToHashtable()` methods for serialization.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1 - Import a single manifest ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Import-DscAdaptedResourceManifest -Path ./MyResource.dsc.adaptedResource.json ```
Imports a single adapted resource manifest and returns a `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` object.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2 - Import all manifests in a directory ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Get-ChildItem -Filter *.dsc.adaptedResource.json | Import-DscAdaptedResourceManifest ```
Imports all adapted resource manifest files in the current directory.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3 - Import and bundle into a manifest list ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Import-DscAdaptedResourceManifest -Path ./MyResource.dsc.adaptedResource.json | New-DscResourceManifest ```
Imports an adapted resource manifest and bundles it into a resource manifest list.

maml:description.maml:para: Imports a DSC resource manifest list from a `.dsc.manifests.json` file.

Reads a `.dsc.manifests.json` file and returns a `DscResourceManifestList` object containing the adapted resources, command-based resources, and extensions defined in the file. This is the inverse of serializing a manifest list with `.ToJson()`.
The adapted resources in the returned list are hydrated into `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` objects. Resources and extensions are stored as hashtables.
The path to a `.dsc.manifests.json` file. Accepts pipeline input.
The path to a `.dsc.manifests.json` file. Accepts pipeline input.
The path to a `.dsc.manifests.json` file, typically piped from `Get-ChildItem`.
Returns a `DscResourceManifestList` object with `.ToJson()` for serialization.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1 - Import a manifest list ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Import-DscResourceManifest -Path ./MyModule.dsc.manifests.json ```
Imports a manifest list file and returns a `DscResourceManifestList` object.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2 - Import all manifest lists in a directory ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Get-ChildItem -Filter *.dsc.manifests.json | Import-DscResourceManifest ```
Imports all manifest list files in the current directory.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3 - Inspect the adapted resources count ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $list = Import-DscResourceManifest -Path ./existing.dsc.manifests.json $list.AdaptedResources.Count ```
Imports a manifest list and inspects the number of adapted resources.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates adapted resource manifest objects from class-based PowerShell DSC resources.

Parses the AST of a PowerShell file (`.ps1`, `.psm1`, or `.psd1`) to find class-based DSC resources decorated with the `[DscResource()]` attribute. For each resource found, it returns a `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` object that complies with the DSCv3 adapted resource manifest JSON schema.

The returned objects can be serialized to JSON using the `.ToJson()` method and written to `.dsc.adaptedResource.json` files. These manifests enable DSCv3 to discover and use PowerShell DSC resources without running `Invoke-DscCacheRefresh`.
When a `.psd1` is provided, the RootModule is resolved and parsed automatically.
The path to a `.ps1`, `.psm1`, or `.psd1` file containing class-based DSC resources. When a `.psd1` is provided, the RootModule is resolved and parsed automatically.
The path to a `.ps1`, `.psm1`, or `.psd1` file containing class-based DSC resources. When a `.psd1` is provided, the RootModule is resolved and parsed automatically.
The path to a PowerShell file, typically piped from `Get-ChildItem`.
Returns a `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` object for each class-based DSC resource found. The object has a `.ToJson()` method for serialization to the adapted resource manifest JSON format.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1 - Generate manifests from a module manifest ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell New-DscAdaptedResourceManifest -Path ./MyModule/MyModule.psd1 ```
Returns adapted resource manifest objects for all class-based DSC resources in the module.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2 - Write each manifest to a JSON file ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: Generates manifest objects and writes each to a JSON file.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3 - Discover and process all modules recursively ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell Get-ChildItem -Path ./MyModules -Filter *.psd1 -Recurse | New-DscAdaptedResourceManifest ```
Discovers all module manifests under `./MyModules` and pipes them into the cmdlet to generate adapted resource manifests for every class-based DSC resource found.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a DscPropertyOverride object for use with Update-DscAdaptedResourceManifest.

Constructs a `DscPropertyOverride` object that specifies how to modify a single property in the embedded JSON schema of an adapted resource manifest. Use this with `Update-DscAdaptedResourceManifest` to customize property descriptions, titles, JSON schema keywords, and required status.
Override the property description text.
A hashtable of JSON schema keywords to merge into the property definition (e.g., `anyOf`, `oneOf`, `default`, `minimum`, `maximum`, `pattern`, `format`).
The name of the property in the embedded JSON schema to override.
An array of JSON schema key names to remove from the property before merging `JsonSchema` (e.g., `'type'`, `'enum'` when replacing with `anyOf`).
Set to `$true` to add the property to the required list, `$false` to remove it, or omit to leave unchanged.
Override the property title text.
Override the property description text.
A hashtable of JSON schema keywords to merge into the property definition (e.g., `anyOf`, `oneOf`, `default`, `minimum`, `maximum`, `pattern`, `format`).
The name of the property in the embedded JSON schema to override.
An array of JSON schema key names to remove from the property before merging `JsonSchema` (e.g., `'type'`, `'enum'` when replacing with `anyOf`).
Set to `$true` to add the property to the required list, `$false` to remove it, or omit to leave unchanged.
Override the property title text.
This cmdlet does not accept pipeline input.
Returns a `DscPropertyOverride` object.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1 - Override a property description ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell New-DscPropertyOverride -Name 'Enabled' -Description 'Whether this resource is active.' ```
Creates an override that sets a custom description for the `Enabled` property.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2 - Replace type/enum with anyOf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: Creates an override that replaces the `type`/`enum` with an `anyOf` schema.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3 - Create multiple overrides and apply them ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $overrides = @( New-DscPropertyOverride -Name 'Name' -Description 'The unique identifier.' New-DscPropertyOverride -Name 'Count' -JsonSchema @{ minimum = 0; maximum = 100 } ) $manifest | Update-DscAdaptedResourceManifest -PropertyOverride $overrides ```
Creates multiple overrides and pipes them to `Update-DscAdaptedResourceManifest`.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a DSC resource manifests list for bundling multiple resources in a single file.

Builds a `DscResourceManifestList` object that can contain both adapted resources and command-based resources. The resulting object can be serialized to JSON and written to a `.dsc.manifests.json` file, which DSCv3 discovers and loads as a bundle.

Adapted resources can be added by piping `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` objects from `New-DscAdaptedResourceManifest`. Command-based resources can be added via the `-Resource` parameter as hashtables matching the DSCv3 resource manifest schema.
One or more `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` objects to include in the manifests list. These are typically produced by `New-DscAdaptedResourceManifest`.

One or more hashtables representing command-based DSC resource manifests. Each hashtable should conform to the DSCv3 resource manifest schema with keys such as `$schema`, `type`, `version`, `get`, `set`, `test`, `schema`, etc.
One or more `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` objects to include in the manifests list. These are typically produced by `New-DscAdaptedResourceManifest`.

One or more hashtables representing command-based DSC resource manifests. Each hashtable should conform to the DSCv3 resource manifest schema with keys such as `$schema`, `type`, `version`, `get`, `set`, `test`, `schema`, etc.
Accepts `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` objects via the pipeline.
Returns a `DscResourceManifestList` object with a `.ToJson()` method for serialization to the `.dsc.manifests.json` format.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1 - Create a manifests list from adapted resources ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $adapted = New-DscAdaptedResourceManifest -Path ./MyModule/MyModule.psd1 New-DscResourceManifest -AdaptedResource $adapted ```
Creates a manifests list from adapted resource manifests generated from a module.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2 - Create a manifests list with a command-based resource ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: Creates a manifests list containing a single command-based resource.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3 - Combine adapted and command-based resources ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: Creates a manifests list combining both adapted and command-based resources.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4 - Pipeline adapted resources directly ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell New-DscAdaptedResourceManifest -Path ./MyModule/MyModule.psd1 | New-DscResourceManifest ```
Pipes adapted resource manifests directly into the cmdlet via the pipeline.

maml:description.maml:para: Applies post-processing overrides to adapted resource manifest objects.

Modifies the embedded JSON schema of a `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` object by applying property-level overrides. This enables customization that AST extraction alone cannot provide, such as meaningful property descriptions, JSON schema keywords like `anyOf` or `oneOf` for complex type unions, default values, numeric ranges, and string patterns.

Property overrides are specified via `DscPropertyOverride` objects that target individual properties by name. Each override can change the description, title, required status, remove existing JSON schema keys, and merge in new JSON schema keywords.
Override the resource-level description on both the manifest object and the embedded JSON schema.
A `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` object to update. Typically produced by `New-DscAdaptedResourceManifest`. Accepts pipeline input.
One or more `DscPropertyOverride` objects specifying modifications to individual properties in the embedded JSON schema. Each override targets a property by `Name`.
Override the resource-level description on both the manifest object and the embedded JSON schema.
A `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` object to update. Typically produced by `New-DscAdaptedResourceManifest`. Accepts pipeline input.
One or more `DscPropertyOverride` objects specifying modifications to individual properties in the embedded JSON schema. Each override targets a property by `Name`.
Accepts `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` objects via the pipeline.
Returns the modified `DscAdaptedResourceManifest` object.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1 - Override a property description ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell New-DscAdaptedResourceManifest -Path ./MyModule/MyModule.psd1 | Update-DscAdaptedResourceManifest -PropertyOverride @( New-DscPropertyOverride -Name 'Name' -Description 'The unique name identifying this resource instance.' ) ```
Overrides the auto-generated description for the `Name` property.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2 - Replace a property schema with anyOf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: Replaces a simple enum property with an `anyOf` schema allowing either a string enum or an integer value.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3 - Add numeric constraints and a default value ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $override = New-DscPropertyOverride -Name 'Count' -JsonSchema @{ minimum = 0; maximum = 100; default = 1 } $manifest | Update-DscAdaptedResourceManifest -PropertyOverride $override ```
Adds numeric constraints and a default value to an existing integer property.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4 - Remove a property from the required list ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell $override = New-DscPropertyOverride -Name 'Tags' -Required $false $manifest | Update-DscAdaptedResourceManifest -PropertyOverride $override ```
Removes a property from the required list.
